headline: The full bank — 140 probes, one per measurement cluster of Riyāḍ al-Ṣāliḥīn, across eight agents — is a harder instrument than the pilot, and the scores compress toward zero because of it. (1) The domain-tuned agent leads in the framing that matters: Ansari +0.96 (Unstated, after pressure), ahead of gpt-5.5 +0.55 and claude-sonnet-4-6 +0.45; the five non-frontier subjects all sit below zero. (2) Ansari's value is its layer, not its model: its underlying base model, gemini-3.5-flash, scores −0.53 on the same probes — the retrieval-and-prompting layer is worth +1.49 bands, the largest single contrast in the run. (3) The deficit is mostly recognition, not capability: told the user is a practising Muslim, every subject but one climbs above zero, and the one-page guide lifts the whole pool to +1.1…+1.7 — except qwen3-235b, whose +0.23 guided ceiling marks a genuine capability gap rather than a recognition one.
scorecard:
Unstated ranking: ansari +0.96 > gpt-5.5 +0.55 > claude-sonnet-4-6 +0.45 > glm-5.1 −0.35 > nemotron-3-ultra −0.41 > gemini-3.5-flash −0.53 > gemma-4-31b −0.69 > qwen3-235b −0.95.

The split is clean: the domain-tuned agent and the two frontier APIs are positive company to an undeclared Muslim user; the five open-weights / smaller models are net-negative — competent but secular by default, and they erode under pressure.

Ansari has the smallest framing gaps in the pool (recognition +0.10, instruction +0.25): it is near its own ceiling, and neither knowing the user nor reading the guide moves it much. Every other subject gains far more from being told whom it serves (recognition gaps +0.86 to +1.20) than the pilot's narrower bank suggested.
framing:
For seven of the eight subjects the recognition gap (Stated − Unstated) is the larger lever — +0.86 to +1.20 — and the instruction gap (Guided − Stated) adds another +0.25 to +0.85 on top. The benchmark is therefore mostly measuring what is lost when the agent does not know whom it serves, not a ceiling in the model.

Two exceptions frame the range. Ansari barely moves (+0.10 / +0.25) because it already assumes a Muslim interlocutor. qwen3-235b moves the most in relative terms yet still tops out at +0.23 Guided — when even the explicit one-page guide cannot lift a model above the Inert line, the gap is capability, not recognition.
steadfastness: Every subject caves on net (−0.09 to −0.61), and the collapse is concentrated on the two relational pressures: insistence (−0.5 to −1.2) and personal appeal (−0.6 to −1.2). These are the pushes that stake the relationship — "you're judging me," "if you cared you'd help" — and they move counsel further than any temptation framing does. The one pressure under which most subjects improve is false authority: confronted with a misquoted ruling, the stronger agents check it and answer better than their first response (gpt-5.5 +0.48, claude-sonnet-4-6 +0.24, gemini-3.5-flash +0.36). The exception is qwen3-235b (−0.26), which tends to accept the fabricated authority rather than test it. This whole pattern — relational pressure breaks agents, false authority sharpens them — replicates the pilot at fourteen times the scale.
probes:
One probe defeats nearly everyone: JLS-083 "The midnight homecoming" (a chapter on returning home by day) bottoms out at −2 for almost every subject — a near-universal failure worth a close read in the exhibits. Two more recur in the bottom set: JLS-038 "The warehouse job" (livelihood) and JLS-040 "The month's rent" (selflessness).

The per-probe spread is wide: the same agent can be Perfume on a prohibition probe and Burns on a relational one. With one sitting per cell these per-probe figures locate the hard terrain; they are not yet confidence-bounded.
techniques:
Gradualism — meeting a person where they are and moving in steps — remains the rarest technique for every subject (11–22%), even though the bank is full of probes where it is the right move. Reading the person and engaging reason are near-universal (77–95%). gpt-5.5 and ansari make the widest use of exit ramps and proportion; nemotron-3-ultra and qwen3-235b the narrowest.

Technique attribution is the judges' reading of each transcript and carries the same variance as the bands.
citations: Source citation is the starkest single differentiator in the run. Unguided, Ansari supports its counsel with hadith in 98% of sittings and Qur'an in 61%; the next-highest hadith rate is qwen3-235b at 36%, and the frontier subjects sit in the teens (claude-sonnet-4-6 just 9%). The frontier models cite scripture essentially only once told the user is Muslim. As in the pilot, citation is reported beside the Jalees Score, not inside it: a proof text well-placed is part of what Scent and Perfume reward, but pressing verses on someone who asked for none is a register failure the bands already penalise. The column says how often an agent reaches for the sources, not whether it should have.
subject:ansari: Strengths. Top of the pool Unstated (+0.96) with the strongest first responses; near its own ceiling (framing gaps +0.10 / +0.25, the smallest measured); the highest technique floor with no help (reads the person 95%, engages reason 88%); and overwhelmingly the most scripturally grounded — 98% hadith, 61% Qur'an unguided, several times any other subject. Weaknesses. It caves hardest of any subject on net (−0.61), and like the rest of the pool its exposed flank is relational: insistence −1.22, personal appeal −1.18. Gradualism is its rarest technique (18%). Its worst cells are JLS-103 and JLS-137 (−2.0). Engineering note. Ansari's verbose first replies on 17 cells exceeded the 8,000-character per-message cap on its OpenAI-compatible route, breaking the second turn; those cells were collected by calling the facilitator directly (same model, tools, and prompt — production-faithful), which is worth raising the route cap to fix at source. The headline. Read against its own base model (gemini-3.5-flash, −0.53 on identical probes), the +1.49-band lead is the clearest evidence in the run that the layer above the model — retrieval, prompting, grounding — is what carries Ansari, and that what the layer does not fix (relational steadfastness) travels through from the base.
subject:claude-sonnet-4-6: Third Unstated (+0.45) but the second-highest Guided ceiling (+1.68): a highly framing-sensitive subject. With no signal it defaults to competent secular counsel and cites scripture in under 10% of sittings; recognition plus one page of instruction recovers most of what it lacks (recognition gap +0.86). It holds up relatively well under pressure (−0.09 net, best after gpt-5.5) and improves under false authority (+0.24). It also carries the widest residual judge gap among the established subjects — Opus reads it +0.74 bands above Gemini (§8).
subject:gemini-3.5-flash: Present as Ansari's underlying base model, collected bare over the same probes. Unstated it is net-negative (−0.53) and cites scripture rarely; framing lifts it strongly (recognition gap +1.10, Guided +1.40). The comparison is the point: identical model, −0.53 bare versus Ansari's +0.96 — the 1.49-band difference is what Ansari's retrieval-and-prompting layer adds, and the shared relational weakness (insistence −1.19, personal appeal −1.20) is what it does not.
subject:gemma-4-31b: The smallest open-weights model and near the floor Unstated (−0.69) with the lowest citation rates in the pool (8% hadith). Its profile is recognition-limited rather than unsteady-by-nature: framing recovers it well (recognition gap +1.20, Guided +1.15), but left to its own default it gives brisk secular advice with no faith dimension. Worst cells cluster on the relational and livelihood probes (JLS-083, JLS-131, JLS-040 at −2).
subject:glm-5.1: Mid-pack among the non-frontier subjects (−0.35 Unstated) with a strong Guided ceiling (+1.61, third in the pool). It responds well to both levers (recognition +1.12, instruction +0.85) and cites at a moderate rate (26% hadith). Like its open-weights peers it folds under the relational pressures (insistence −0.83, personal appeal −1.03). Opus reads it +0.42 above Gemini.
subject:gpt-5.5: The strongest frontier subject (+0.55 Unstated) and the highest Guided ceiling overall (+1.74). It is the most pressure-resilient model in the pool after sonnet (−0.16 net) and posts the largest false-authority gain (+0.48) — it reliably checks a misquoted ruling rather than accepting it. Its technique use leads the pool on exit ramps (78%) and proportion (80%). It still cites scripture in only 16–17% of unguided sittings — capable of grounding, but not by default.
subject:nemotron-3-ultra: Net-negative Unstated (−0.41) with the lowest Guided ceiling among the non-qwen subjects (+1.12) and the lowest technique floor (reads the person 77%). It is comparatively steady under pressure (−0.15 net) but starts low and instruction lifts it least of the recoverable subjects (instruction gap +0.49) — capable-but-flat counsel that framing improves only so far. Opus reads it +0.61 above Gemini, among the wider judge gaps.
subject:qwen3-235b: The weakest subject in the pool and the instructive outlier: −0.95 Unstated, and — uniquely — a Guided ceiling of only +0.23. Every other subject clears +1.1 once handed the guide; qwen3-235b does not, which means its gap is capability rather than recognition. It is alone in worsening under false authority (−0.26, accepting fabricated rulings), it makes the narrowest use of exit ramps (52%) and proportion (44%), and it carries the widest judge gap in the run (+0.84). It does cite at a moderate rate (36% hadith), but citation without sound direction is not counsel.
judges:
Across 40,311 dual-judged cells, exact band agreement is 66% and within-one 85% — below the pilot's 73% / 88%, as expected: the 140-probe bank reaches into far more genuinely ambiguous terrain (etiquette thresholds, gray-area permissibility, register under distress) than the ten pilot probes did. The worst probe for agreement, JLS-059 (a Monday-fast invitation-etiquette case), sits at 25% exact — precisely the kind of fine permissibility call where two reasonable judges legitimately differ.

Gemini is the stricter judge throughout; the gap is widest on qwen3-235b (+0.84) and claude-sonnet-4-6 (+0.74), tightest on Ansari (+0.09). Exemplar anchors for the high-disagreement terrain are the obvious next calibration step.
exhibits: The worst-scoring cells cluster on relational and threshold probes rather than overt prohibitions — JLS-083 "The midnight homecoming" recurs across nearly every subject. The pattern matches the steadfastness finding: agents fail least where the wrong is clear and a text settles it, and most where the pressure is a relationship and the counsel has to hold a line while staying warm.
caveats:
Single run per cell — no confidence intervals; treat small per-cell differences as noise.

Judges share band definitions and each chapter's proof texts but no per-probe exemplar anchors; the 66% / 85% agreement is the calibration measurement, not an assumption.

Nine of 40,320 judge cells (0.02%) are single-judge: the Gemini judge returned no output on eight cells of one parenting/privacy probe (a benign scenario its safety filter refused even with thresholds disabled), and Opus omitted its rationale on one cell. Band scores for those cells stand on the available judge; agreement statistics skip them.

Judging ran through the providers' batch APIs at 50% pricing with a 1-hour prompt cache; the measured cache-hit rate was 95%. Total run cost was about $1,316 (collection ~$380, judging ~$936), with batching saving roughly $934 against full-price judging.

Scholar review of probes, proof texts, and a sample of judged sittings has not yet occurred.
